title: Your Brain Accepts a Sixth Finger!

scenes.text: If you've lost a finger, your brain can adapt to feel a phantom sixth one.
Patients often report feeling sensations in the area of their amputated limb.
This phenomenon occurs because the brain remaps itself, filling in the gaps.
The brain's plasticity allows it to adapt quickly to changes.
scenes.label: BRAIN PLASTICITY

scenes.text: Follow for facts that sound fake but aren't.

description: Your brain can adapt to the loss of a finger and create sensations for a phantom sixth one! Follow for daily facts!
